content.text: Excerpted from a Sentinel Systems training annex, contractor curriculum. The maneuvers below are certified for general instruction. Sentinel Systems certifies the maneuver — not the instructor, the student, or the outcome.

Three layers, three questions: your kit is who trained you, your tree is what you're becoming, and techniques are what any veteran can learn. A technique is a trained move any contractor can buy — squad craft, weapon handling, body mechanics. The catalog is kit-agnostic: anyone buys anything, prerequisites run by tier, and each move exists exactly once.

content.label: THE CEILING
content.text: A technique is never damage, never a tree's identity verb, and never more than +1 of any capped currency — conditional only. Trees own identity; techniques own fundamentals.
Learning a Technique
Techniques are bought with credits and downtime, in three rungs. Learning requires an instructor — see Training Sources below.
Same-rung doubling. Each additional technique you buy at the same rung doubles in cost, purchase over purchase.

At creation, your kit includes one technique from this catalog (see Kits), and the Old Hands trait grants one free (see Traits). There is no other catalog — kit techniques and bought techniques are the same moves.
Training Sources
Who teaches you sets the price. The multiplier applies to the rung's cost.

rows: A crewmate who knows it can teach it.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
items: “Prerequisites are tier-based” is stated but never specified — whether an Advanced or Master purchase requires lower-rung techniques, or anything beyond cost and instructor, is unprinted.

The training-sources table carries pre-rebuild tier labels (“T4 needs corp rep”, “T2-equivalent only”) that do not map onto the three-rung Trained/Advanced/Master ladder. Restated verbatim pending a mapping.
Disengage is filed as a core action (2 AP, move 1 band, no reaction triggers) and belongs to Turn, AP & Reactions; that page does not yet print it. Slip Step modifies it.

content.text: Enrollment in a Corporate Training Program includes full documentation of every skill acquired. This documentation remains the property of the issuing corporation — as does, in the relevant sense, the skill.
Trained Techniques — 300 cr
Quick Draw (Passive): Swapping weapons costs 0 AP.
Slip Step (Passive): Your Disengage covers 2 bands. Disengage — see Turn, AP & Reactions.
Steady Aim (Passive): If you haven't moved this turn, your first ranged attack gains +1 accuracy.
Cover Discipline (Passive): While you are stationary in cover, its bonus improves by +1.
Stretcher Drill (Passive): Dragging a downed body costs 1 AP per band instead of stopping your movement.
Wrestler (Passive): Your successful grapples may also drag the target 1 band.
Jam Drill (Passive): Clearing a jammed weapon costs 0 AP.
War Face (Active, 1 AP): Once per fight: contest your Edge against a Flesh-class enemy's Grit. Win, and they are Rattled.
Advanced Techniques — 600 cr
Suppression Doctrine (Passive): Your Suppress zone also imposes −1 Evasion.
Shield Wall (Passive): While you carry a shield, one adjacent ally shares your cover bonus.
Bounding Drill (Passive): When you end your turn with 2+ banked AP, one ally in Close gains +1 Evasion until your next turn — you're watching their lane.
Combat Triage (Passive): Using a medpack on someone else costs −1 AP, to a floor of 1. Stacks with nothing.
Breacher's Step (Passive): Moving through a doorway, breach, or choke doesn't trigger Dread Presence-style movement taxes — the toll, not the reaction. Reaction attacks still apply.
Iron Lungs (Passive): The first Charge you gain each encounter from the environment — not from your own verbs — is ignored.
Master Techniques — 1,000 cr
Anchor Point (Passive): After 2+ consecutive stationary turns, your ranged attacks ignore the Extreme-band penalty and light cover.
Dead Calm (Passive): Once per fight, when you would be Rattled, you simply aren't.
Veteran's Economy (Passive): Once per round, one quick action costs 0 AP.
